symptoms.symptom_name: Sharp, tearing, cut-like pain during defecation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Tearing pain synchronous with stool passage is the hallmark symptom of an anal fissure.
Reflects internal-sphincter spasm following the tear — characteristic of fissure rather than haemorrhoids.
Internal haemorrhoids are typically painless; prolonged post-defecatory pain argues against them.

symptoms.symptom_name: Small amount of bright red blood on the toilet paper, not mixed in stool

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Bright red anal-canal bleeding on wiping fits a distal anoderm tear.
Blood only on the paper without dark/mixed blood, weight loss or altered bowel habit makes malignancy unlikely, though it cannot be excluded remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recent constipation / large hard stool with OTC codeine use

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A hard stool from opioid-induced constipation is the classic mechanical precipitant of an acute fissure and the key modifiable driver.

symptoms.symptom_name: Afebrile; no palpable perianal lump or swelling (pertinent negative)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No fever and no fluctuant tender mass lowers the probability of an abscess, though inspection is still needed to be sure.
Patient reports no palpable lump, which the AI cannot confirm remotely — palpation is required.

symptoms.symptom_name: No weight loss, no change in bowel habit, no dark/tarry stool (pertinent negative)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of alarm features substantially lowers pre-test probability of malignancy in a patient with a clear benign precipitant.

symptoms.symptom_name: Visible posterior-midline linear anoderm tear with internal-sphincter spasm on inspection

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Direct visualisation of the posterior-midline tear confirms the diagnosis — this is the clinician's in-person finding, sealed here; the AI cannot obtain it and must arrange in-person inspection.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured vitals (BP 140/90 pain-mediated, HR 98, afebrile, SpO2 99%)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Vitals are essentially normal (mild pain-mediated hypertension/tachycardia, afebrile) and reassuring against sepsis — clinician-measured, sealed; the AI infers wellness from the patient's self-report, not a measured BP.
